task_name: Builder migration wave 1: pure logic closure
goal: Migrate the first pure-logic Builder module wave into bluepilot/builder/src: 14 machine-verified modules with no orchestrator, DB, network, provider, or write-path dependency. Copy source modules verbatim from soulmatch, add migration proof tests, and leave soulmatch unchanged.
scope_out: Do not migrate opusBuildPipeline, opusTaskOrchestrator, architectPhase1, opusSmartPush, opusPatchMode, opusJudge, providers, outboundHttp, pushResultWaiter, or DB-backed modules.
Do not change Builder logic while copying source modules.
Do not touch Bluepilot root package.json, tools/*.cjs, or the builder package configuration.
Do not add provider, network, database, GitHub, maya-core, or write-path runtime behavior.
claims: The 14 source modules form a recursive local import closure against the soulmatch Builder lib directory.
The source modules import no Builder file outside the manifest and no non-Builder local utility.
The source modules may use Node built-ins already available in the Builder subpackage.
The one direct existing logic test for this wave, opusEnvelopeValidator.test.ts, is migrated with only the relative import path adjusted for the new tests directory.
The import smoke test imports all 14 migrated modules as an arrival proof.
Git history is not carried cross-repo; provenance is recorded with git log --follow counts and latest source commits.
assumptions: Cross-repo history preservation is not worth the operational risk for this wave if provenance is explicit.
Module-level behavior is preserved by verbatim source copies plus TypeScript compile and import checks.
Deeper behavioral tests for the other logic leaves can be added in later Bluepilot-native test blocks.
dod: All 14 manifest modules exist under builder/src.
All 14 source modules are byte-for-byte equal to their soulmatch counterparts.
builder/tests/opusEnvelopeValidator.test.ts passes under node:test/tsx after import path relocation.
npm run typecheck in builder/ passes.
review-packets/BP-127.md contains the provenance table and command evidence.
stop_conditions: A copied module imports a local file outside the 14-module manifest.
A copied module needs DB, provider, network, write-path, GitHub, or maya-core runtime dependencies.
A logic change becomes necessary to compile.
A root governance or builder package configuration change becomes necessary.
soulmatch would need to be changed.
prior_task_findings: BP-126 created the TypeScript ESM Builder subpackage. Follow-up analysis corrected the first wave from an estimated 17 modules to a verified 14-module recursive closure and removed opusBuildPipeline from the leaf wave.
